title: carramboa littlei
side_effects: Carramboa littlei can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, including itchy skin, hives, and swelling. Ingesting the plant's sap can cause nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, while prolonged exposure to its oils may lead to respiratory issues like coughing and shortness of breath. Furthermore, improper use of the Carramboa littlei plant has been linked to skin irritation, including redness and burns, especially when applied topically without adequate dilution or preparation. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can even trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
